title: Remove Counterbalance Weights From the Tipper
summary: Safely remove the tipper counterbalance weights and associated fasteners using the documented removal procedure. The source states that two people are required, the tipper must be locked out/tagged out, guarding may need to be removed for access, four M10 socket head screws with lock washers secure the weights, and the carriage may drift so that the gripper moves to the lower hard stop after the weights are removed.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when service work requires removal of the tipper counterbalance weights as documented in section 7.3.3.1 Removal of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use for general operator tasks.
Do not use when the required two-person handling support is not available.
Do not use as a substitute for the separate Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure referenced by the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform the lockout/tagout, access the counterbalance area, remove the fasteners, control the weight during removal, and verify the resulting carriage movement matches the documented acceptable condition.
Provide second-person assistance during handling and control of the approximately 20 kg (44 lb) total counterbalance weight during fastener removal and lift-off.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe for general operator use.
Two people are required by the source.
Perform the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before accessing or removing the counterbalance weights.
Guarding may need to be removed to gain access.
The counterbalance weights total about 20 kg (44 lb).
As the fourth fastener is removed, hold the weights so they do not fall.
After removal, the carriage may begin to move; the documented acceptable result is that the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.

access_required: Access to the tipper counterbalance weight area
system_preconditions: Two people are available for the procedure
The tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2

visual_references.description: Figure 7-4 Counterbalance Weight showing the counterbalance weight assembly, fasteners, and mounting area referenced by the removal procedure.

steps.title: Prepare personnel, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure the documented staffing, PPE, and tools are in place before beginning removal.
steps.instruction: Use two people for the procedure and gather the documented PPE and tools: safety glasses, gloves, 16-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.
steps.expected_result: Two qualified people are present and the required PPE and tools are available at the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Both people are ready to perform the task and the listed PPE and tools are available.
steps.failure_condition: A second person is not available or the documented PPE/tools are missing.

actions.label: Confirm two-person requirement
actions.exact_action: Use two people for the procedure.

actions.label: Gather PPE and tools
actions.exact_action: Gather safety glasses, gloves, 16-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the counterbalance weight assembly location to understand the work area before starting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required PPE or tools are not available.

source_evidence.value: The source states that two people are required.
Use two people for the procedure and gather the documented PPE and tools: safety glasses, gloves, 16-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench/socket.
The nearby procedure states that two people are required, with tools including a 16-mm hex wrench and 3/8-in. wrench/socket, and PPE including safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the tipper in the required safe maintenance state before accessing the counterbalance weights.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out/tagged out in accordance with the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper is in the documented LOTO state before any guarding or fasteners are removed.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out/tagged out or the referenced LOTO procedure cannot be completed.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tipper cannot be placed in the required LOTO state.
The referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is unavailable.

source_evidence.value: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2.
The source references a separate Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 but does not restate those steps here.
The figure is associated with removal of the counterbalance weights by first performing LOTO on the tipper.

steps.title: Remove guarding if needed for access
steps.purpose: Gain access to the counterbalance weights when the top and/or bottom side guarding blocks access.
steps.instruction: Remove the top and/or bottom side guarding if necessary to access the counterbalance weights.
steps.expected_result: The counterbalance weight area is accessible for fastener removal.
steps.healthy_condition: Guarding is removed only as needed and the counterbalance weight mounting area is visible and reachable.
steps.failure_condition: The counterbalance weight area remains inaccessible after the necessary guarding is addressed.

actions.label: Remove guarding for access
actions.exact_action: Remove the top and/or bottom side guarding if necessary to access the counterbalance weights.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-4 to identify the counterbalance weight area and the guarding/access area around the assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required access to the counterbalance weights cannot be obtained.
Guarding removal exposes conditions not covered by the source procedure.

source_evidence.value: Remove the top and/or bottom side guarding if necessary to access the counterbalance weights. [Image support needed: figure showing the counterbalance weight area and guarding that may need removal for access.]
The figure is associated with removal of the counterbalance weights by first performing LOTO on the tipper, removing top and/or bottom side guarding if necessary.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-4 appears on page 111 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_3_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the four counterbalance fasteners
steps.purpose: Release the counterbalance weights from their mounting points.
steps.instruction: Remove the four M10 socket head screws holding the counterbalance weights in place, including the lock washers.
steps.expected_result: All four M10 socket head screws and their lock washers are removed from the counterbalance weight assembly.
steps.healthy_condition: The four documented fasteners and lock washers are removed and the weights are ready to be supported and lifted away.
steps.failure_condition: One or more fasteners cannot be removed or the weights cannot be safely controlled during fastener removal.

actions.label: Remove M10 screws and lock washers
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M10 socket head screws holding the counterbalance weights in place, including the lock washers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the counterbalance weights, the four M10 socket head screws, lock washers, and mounting points shown in Figure 7-4.

stop_or_escalate_if: The weights cannot be controlled during fastener removal.

source_evidence.value: Remove the four M10 socket head screws holding the counterbalance weights in place, including the lock washers. [Image support needed: Figure 7-4 showing the counterbalance weights, four M10 socket head screws, lock washers, and mounting points.]
The figure is associated with removal of the counterbalance weights by ... removing four M10 socket head screws with lock washers holding the counterbalances in place.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-4 appears on page 111 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_3_1_removal.

steps.title: Support the weights during final fastener removal
steps.purpose: Prevent the counterbalance weights from falling when the final fastener is removed.
steps.instruction: As the fourth fastener is removed, hold the weights so they do not fall; the source states the weights are about 20 kg (44 lb) total.
steps.expected_result: The weights remain controlled and do not fall as the final fastener is removed.
steps.healthy_condition: The approximately 20 kg (44 lb) total weight is fully controlled by the two-person team during final release.
steps.failure_condition: The weights shift unexpectedly, cannot be controlled, or are at risk of falling.

actions.label: Support weights at final release
actions.exact_action: As the fourth fastener is removed, hold the weights so they do not fall.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-4 to confirm the weight assembly orientation and where support is needed during final fastener removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The weights cannot be controlled during removal.
The weights begin to fall or shift unexpectedly.

source_evidence.value: As the fourth fastener is removed, hold the weights so they do not fall; the source states the weights are about 20 kg (44 lb) total.
Stop if the weights cannot be controlled during removal or if the observed movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
The text warns that the weights total about 20 kg (44 lb).

steps.title: Remove the weights and verify acceptable carriage drift
steps.purpose: Complete removal of the counterbalance weights and verify the resulting movement matches the documented acceptable condition.
steps.instruction: Remove the counterbalance weights and observe that the carriage may begin to move; the documented acceptable result is that the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.
steps.expected_result: The counterbalance weights are removed from the tipper, and any resulting carriage movement is limited to the documented acceptable drift of the gripper to the lower hard stop.
steps.healthy_condition: After the weights are removed, the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.
steps.failure_condition: Observed movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.

actions.label: Observe carriage movement
actions.exact_action: Observe that the carriage may begin to move; the documented acceptable result is that the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-4 as the reference for the counterbalance weight assembly being removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observed movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.

source_evidence.value: Remove the counterbalance weights and observe that the carriage may begin to move; the documented acceptable result is that the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.
The counterbalance weights are removed from the tipper, and any resulting carriage movement is limited to the documented acceptable drift of the gripper to the lower hard stop.
Stop if the weights cannot be controlled during removal or if the observed movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
The text warns that ... the carriage may drift to the lower hard stop after removal.

success_criteria: The tipper counterbalance weights are removed.
All four M10 socket head screws and lock washers have been removed from the weight assembly.
The weights are controlled during removal and do not fall.
Any resulting carriage movement matches the documented acceptable condition: the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.
healthy_conditions: Two people are used for the procedure.
The tipper is locked out/tagged out before access and removal work begins.
The counterbalance weight area is accessible after any necessary guarding removal.
The approximately 20 kg (44 lb) total weight is controlled during final fastener removal.
After removal, the gripper drifts to the lower hard stop.
failure_conditions: The procedure is attempted without two people.
The tipper is not locked out/tagged out.
The counterbalance weights cannot be safely controlled during removal.
The weights fall or are at risk of falling during final fastener removal.
Observed carriage movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
escalation_guidance: Stop the procedure if the weights cannot be controlled during removal.
Stop and escalate if the observed movement does not match the documented acceptable drift to the lower hard stop.
Escalate if the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure cannot be completed or is unavailable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counterbalance weight assembly, fastener locations, lock washers, and mounting points referenced by the removal procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-4 appears on page 111 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_3_1_removal.
